title: Treat the cache as derived and lossy
description: Every cache read must tolerate a miss by falling back to the source, since Redis can evict or restart anytime.
body: Use when reading any value from Redis used as a cache. Do not treat a cache hit as the system of record or assume a key you wrote is still present. Actions: on miss, read from the source of truth, repopulate with a TTL, and return; make the source path correct on its own so a cold or flushed cache only costs latency, not correctness. Validation signals include a test that flushing the cache still yields correct results from the source and repopulates. Risk boundary: code that errors or returns wrong data on a cache miss has made the cache load-bearing for correctness — a blocking design finding, since eviction and restarts are normal.
